title: The silver eva
wikitext:
[]: The silver eva?             Quote Post by terminus » Fri Jan 26, 2007 11:46 pm well I frogot what episode it was in but, there was a silver eva unit shown for like a second then it blew up, is their any more info about this eva?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 27, 2007 1:02 am Eva-04, but it was never shown in the anime. Official art, however, has portrayed it as a silver/white version of Eva-03.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jan 27, 2007 10:26 am Leakypedia says it appears in various NGE spin-offs, but I don't remember any specific instances.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 27, 2007 11:14 am BobBQ wrote: Leakypedia says it appears in various NGE spin-offs, but I don't remember any specific instances.

[]:             Quote Post by terminus » Sat Jan 27, 2007 11:53 am o wow it looks awsome, too bad it blew up so early

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 27, 2007 1:15 pm 04 is just 03 with the chrome finish rather than the midnight blue.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 27, 2007 1:31 pm terminus wrote: o wow it looks awsome, too bad it blew up so early

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Jan 27, 2007 1:37 pm Mr. Tines wrote: 04 is just 03 with the chrome finish rather than the midnight blue.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 27, 2007 1:48 pm BobBQ wrote: And powered by the soul of Madame MacGuffin.

[]:             Quote Post by Kyle » Sat Jan 27, 2007 11:54 pm They had an action figure of unit-4 didnt they?

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Sun Jan 28, 2007 2:45 pm Kyle wrote: They had an action figure of unit-4 didnt they?

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Sun Jan 28, 2007 9:18 pm well, they only speculated that it was pulled into a dirac sea. the actual lines from the show: IBUKI: Evangelion Unit 04 and all related research facilities within an 89km radius have completely vanished. RITSUKO: Along with several thousand people. AOBA: Looking at the time schedule, we believe it to be an accident during the experimental installation of the S2 engine that was restored in Germany. IBUKI: Potential causes range from a lack of structural integrity to a preliminary design stage error. That's 32,768 possibilities right there. Misato (OFF): And there's also the chance of sabotage. HYUGA: But it didn't explode, it vanished, right? In other words, it disappeared. RITSUKO: In all probability, they were swallowed by a Sea of Dirac, just like Unit 01 was. Misato: And the S2 engine that we finally restored? RITSUKO: Gone. Our dream's been shattered.
